page: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARM64-based AWS Graviton processor utilizing the ARMv8 Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. The instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores with 8 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz with no simultaneous multithreading, meaning there is exactly 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Cache specifications include 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 184.3 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 2959.48, both placing in the top 25% tier. However, raw computational benchmarks show some limitations; the PassMark CPU score of 6073.06 and the PassMark CPU encryption test score of 1104.87 fall into the bottom 25% tier. Other processing metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1497.0 and multi-core score of 8171.0, align with average tier performance. Redis SET operations achieve an average throughput of 13.53 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 15.88 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds vary, with 135M model text generation and 7B model prompt processing registering in the bottom 10% tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7g.2xlarge leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high memory density per core without the overhead of hyperthreading. This design provides predictable execution times for memory-bound workloads. Given the strong memory bandwidth and average database operation performance of 8026.68 on PassMark, this instance is suited for memory-intensive applications, caching layers such as Redis, and database deployments that do not require local NVMe storage. It is less suited for intensive cryptographic operations or heavy LLM text generation due to lower benchmarked performance in those specific areas.
description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core with dedicated CPU allocation and no hyperthreading. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results highlight strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 184.3 GB/sec placing in the top 25% tier. Conversely, cryptographic and raw CPU benchmarks, such as the PassMark CPU score, rank in the bottom 25%. This profile makes the r7g.2xlarge suited for memory-intensive workloads, caching layers like Redis, and database operations, while being less optimal for compute-heavy or cryptographic tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7g.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, showing strong memory bandwidth benchmarks.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth for memory-intensive workloads and caching.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 184.3 GB/sec
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for memory-intensive applications, databases, and Redis caching
Lower performance in cryptographic and raw CPU-bound benchmarks
